content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of **N** integer numbers **A**. Calculate the sum of **Ai AND Aj** for all the pairs (**i**, **j**) where **i < j**. 

 The **AND** operation is the **Bitwise AND** operation, defined as in here.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (the length of the sequence). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the sum of (Ai AND Aj) over all pairs i < j.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Every AND is 0, so the total sum is 0.
Each pair yields 7. Number of pairs = C(4,2)=6. 6*7 = 42.

hidden.label: general case 2 with powers of two

solution.approach: For each bit position (0 … 20) count how many numbers have this bit set. If cnt numbers contain the bit, then exactly C(cnt,2) pairs contribute 1<<bit to the final sum because the AND of a pair has this bit set only when both numbers have it. Sum the contributions of all bits. This runs in O(N * B) time where B = 21 (enough for Ai ≤ 1e6) and O(1) extra space.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it)
        a = [next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        ans = 0
        for bit in range(21):
            mask = 1 << bit
            cnt = 0
            for v in a:
                if v & mask:
                    cnt += 1
            ans += (cnt * (cnt - 1) // 2) * mask
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('
'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
